MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT LANNY JAY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist. See Plaintiff's Separate Statement pages 5 through 24.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: K.Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File An Amendment To The Answer Of Defendant Lanny Jay
Off Calendar Aug-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LANNY JAY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDMENT TO ANSWER

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. The lack of a certificate of occupancy is not a valid defense against a tenant's habitability or harassment claims, accordingly the amendment would be futile. The defense is also subsumed in the "In Pari Delicto" defense asserted in the existing answer. Defendant's proposed defense regarding SFRO 37.9(f) is not an affirmative defense as phrased, as it does not bring in a "new matter that is not responsive to the essential allegations in the plaintiff's complaint." (The Bank of New York Mellon v. Preciado (2013) 224 Cal.App.4th Supp. 1, 8.) It is also already subsumed in the "failure to state a claim" defense asserted in the existing answer. Defendant's request to amend their Answer to include attorney's fees is denied. Defendant may seek attorney fees after judgment is authorized by law. Amending the Answer is unnecessary. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Two Chairs And/Or Motion For Protective Order, Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ISABELLA SERRANO's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Two Chairs And/Or Motion For Protective Order, Request For Sanctions is GRANTED.

Defendants seek the production of all business and medical records pertaining to Plaintiff's treatment at counseling center, Two Chairs. Plaintiff asserts this request is overbroad and raises the psychotherapist-patient privilege and constitutional privacy interests in response.

Disclosure of a party's mental health records can only be compelled when the party has put their mental condition at issue. (In re Lifschutz (1970) 2 Cal.3d 415.) "The psychotherapist-patient privilege is a kind of privacy interest that may be overcome only on a showing of a compelling state interest." (Kirchmeyer v. Phillips (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 1394, 1404.) On this record, Plaintiff's garden-variety emotional distress allegations do not justify this discovery. Moreover, Defendants' request is overbroad, in that it seeks "any and all other psychiatric records and materials," not just those relating to her treatment for emotional distress caused by her tenancy. (Wheeler decl. Ex. 1.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts' ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: John True; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/JT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Kaiser Permanente And/Or Motion For Protective Order, Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ISABELLA SERRANO's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Kaiser Permanente And/Or Motion For Protective Order, Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiff Isabella Serrano's motion to quash deposition subpoena for production of business records as to Kaiser Permanente is GRANTED.

Defendants seek the production of all business and medical records pertaining to Plaintiff's medical treatment from Kaiser Permanente. (Wheeler decl. Ex. 1.) Plaintiff asserts this request is overbroad and raises the physician-patient privilege and constitutional privacy interests in response.

Plaintiff was a tenant at the subject property form 2008 until around 2024. She claims that habitability issues with the property caused her to suffer asthma and breathing difficulties, and that she received treatment for those injuries at Kaiser. (Defendants' request for judicial notice, Ex. 2, Response to Form Interrogatory 6.2, 6.4.) Yet Defendant seeks all of Plaintiff's medical records from Kaiser, exceeding the duration of Plaintiff's tenancy or type of treatment she received. This request is overbroad, and on this record, Defendants' need for this discovery is outweighed by Plaintiff's privacy concerns.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts' ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: John True; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/JT)
